doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_div: divide two MPFR values at the caller-supplied prec, rounded per rnd, returning {value, ternary}. The C function mutates rop in place (whose prec is set independently) and returns the ternary as int; the TS port takes prec explicitly and returns the canonical Result shape from src/core.ts. Class is 'arithmetic' (50ms per-case budget). The general path computes the bigint quotient num/b.mant where num = a.mant << X with X chosen so the quotient carries prec+1 bits — one round bit and a remainder-derived sticky. The round bit is the LSB of the (prec+1)-bit quotient; the sticky is (remainder !== 0n). The bit-length of the quotient is either prec or prec+1 depending on whether a.mant > b.mant or a.mant <= b.mant respectively (in the MSB-aligned framing). Two normalisation branches feed roundMantissa with srcPrec = prec+1 and an srcExp computed from a.exp - b.exp + normShift where normShift in {0, 1}. The fuzz cap of prec <= 200 keeps the bigint divisions comfortably inside the 50ms budget.
c_signature: int mpfr_div(mpfr_t rop, mpfr_srcptr op1, mpfr_srcptr op2, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
divergence_from_c: C mutates rop (which carries prec) and returns the ternary as a separate int; TS takes prec as a positional argument and returns {value, ternary} per the locked Result schema (src/core.ts L173-L176). Behaviour for specials matches the C reference (mpfr/src/div.c L783-L831): NaN / anything -> NaN; Inf / Inf -> NaN; 0 / 0 -> NaN; Inf / finite -> sign-product Inf, ternary 0; finite / Inf -> sign-product zero, ternary 0; finite / 0 -> sign-product Inf (divbyzero), ternary 0; 0 / finite-nonzero -> sign-product zero, ternary 0. Normal / normal: result sign = a.sign * b.sign (sign-product, NOT a.sign); quotient via bigint shift-and-divide with explicit round/sticky tracking; result exp = a.exp - b.exp + (L - 1) where L is the bit-length of the (prec+1)-bit quotient; then roundMantissa drops one bit using the remainder as sticky.
refs: mpfr/src/div.c L740-L848 — top-level dispatcher (mpfr_div). L783-L831 is the singular dispatch (MPFR_ARE_SINGULAR); the rest is the prec-class fast paths (mpfr_div_1/2/1n) which the TS port collapses into one general bigint path. The general algorithm (L865+, post-fast-paths) is what we mirror: usize/vsize limb counts, a divisor-side comparison cmp(up, vp) which corresponds to our 'a.mant > b.mant' detection, and an explicit round_bit/sticky_u/sticky_v tracking that we reproduce as 'roundBit' and '(remainder !== 0n)' on the bigint side.

mpfr/src/div.c L783-L831 — singular dispatch. NaN inputs -> NaN; (Inf/Inf, 0/0) -> NaN; Inf/finite -> sign-product Inf; finite/Inf -> sign-product zero; finite/0 -> sign-product Inf (with the divbyzero flag, which the TS port silently follows MPFR's spec on but does not surface — the result IS the correct +-Inf, which is what the value-only contract demands). 0/finite-nonzero -> sign-product zero. The sign of every special non-NaN branch is MPFR_MULT_SIGN(MPFR_SIGN(u), MPFR_SIGN(v)) — the same product-of-signs rule as mpfr_mul.

mpfr/src/div_1.c, div_2.c, div_1n.c — prec-class fast paths. Reference for the round/sticky decomposition under explicit limb arithmetic. The TS port is faithful to their I/O contract via a single bigint division.
src/core.ts — locked MPFR / RoundingMode / Result / Ternary types; validate() invariants the returned value must satisfy.

src/ops/mul.ts — reference for the arithmetic-class structure (specials dispatch then a normal-normal core function), the product-of-signs rule, and the packNormal/roundMantissa pipeline. mpfr_div mirrors mul.ts almost line-by-line at the dispatcher level; the algebraic core diverges (division replaces multiplication, with explicit shift-and-remainder for the round/sticky bits).

src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts — the substrate primitive used to round the (prec+1)-bit quotient down to prec bits. We synthesise the sticky-augmented bit by ORing the remainder-nonzero flag into the LSB-below-round position before invoking roundMantissa with srcPrec=prec+1 — the same trick add.ts uses for the cancellation-shifted bigint, and the same trick mul.ts uses for the bigint product.
mpfr/tests/tdiv.c — source for the mined cases. Specifically check4 invocations at known boundaries.

CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — 0/0 == NaN; Inf/Inf == NaN; ternary direction is sign of (rounded - exact); rounding-mode count is FIVE; the sign rule for div is product-of-signs (not a.sign as in same-sign add). mpfr_prec_t in bits not digits.
